meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional apps at lightning speed.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project stagnation is decision fatigue, not lack of skill. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you eliminate boilerplate overhead. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for automated implementation, you convert hours of repetitive setup into minutes of high-value, deployable feature development.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the client brief is signed, the excitement is palpable, but three weeks later, you’re still wrestling with authentication middleware and database schemas. You’re drowning in the 'middle-project slump'—that graveyard where half-finished SaaS projects go to die. It’s not a lack of talent; it’s a lack of leverage.

Professional shipping isn't about writing every line of code from scratch; it's about composing high-quality primitives into a scalable machine. By adopting a standardized SaaS starter kit, you move from being a 'coder' to an 'architect.' Let’s replace your friction with a repeatable, automated pipeline that turns your architectural vision into a production-ready reality.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a robust project structure that handles auth, database, and UI primitives out of the box.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-grade SaaS boilerplate including Next.js 15, Drizzle ORM, and Lucia Auth. Enforce strict TypeScript and Tailwind CSS patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure using the standard SaaS starter kit template'

steps.title: Define Domain Entities
steps.description: Map your client's business logic to database schemas using AI-assisted schema generation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Drizzle schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application with plans, subscriptions, and user-role management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Drizzle schemas for tenants, users, and subscription plans in src/db/schema.ts'

steps.title: Implement API Endpoints
steps.description: Automate the creation of type-safe API routes that interact with your database schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create CRUD API handlers for subscription management using Zod validation and server actions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement server actions for subscription CRUD based on existing schema'

steps.title: Bootstrap UI Components
steps.description: Use your starter kit's design system to rapidly assemble high-fidelity dashboard interfaces.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard layout using Shadcn UI components that maps to the existing API endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Assemble dashboard UI components using existing Shadcn library'

steps.title: Automate Testing & Linting
steps.description: Ensure your project remains stable by automating quality checks during every commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions that runs linting, type-checking, and integration tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup GitHub Actions for automated linting and testing pipeline'

pro_tips: Always maintain a 'golden branch' of your starter kit; never modify the boilerplate directly, always create feature branches.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store common client-specific architectural constraints to avoid repeating instructions.
Prioritize 'feature flagging' early; it allows you to ship incomplete modules to production without breaking the UX.
Standardize your database migration workflow to ensure local, staging, and production environments never drift.
Treat your starter kit as a product; document your own custom components so you can reuse them across multiple client projects.
content.next_steps: Once your environment is set up, focus on deploying to a provider like Vercel or Railway. Create a 'Project Checklist' template to track your progress and ensure you never miss a launch requirement again.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the blank page and start shipping the software your clients deserve. Use these tools to reclaim your time, double your velocity, and build a reputation for reliability. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
